summary: Jeff Atwood shares his discovery of an 'I Rock at BASIC' t-shirt featuring the classic infinite loop program that prints a message forever. The post is a brief, nostalgic nod to the shared experience of learning to program, suggesting that writing this simple endless loop was a rite of passage for programmers of a certain generation. It celebrates the humble beginnings that many developers share.
key_points: The infinite GOTO loop printing a message is a universal first program for BASIC programmers
There's a shared cultural touchstone among developers who started with BASIC
Programming nostalgia has commercial appeal (the t-shirt)
money_quotes: We've all written this program at some point in our careers.
But only those of us who truly rock at BASIC.
key_insight: The simplest programs from our coding origins—like an infinite BASIC loop—become shared badges of honor in developer culture.
